seo.title: AI Agent Cost Optimization in AG2 Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent cost optimization in AG2 agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In AG2, AI Agent Cost Optimization issues manifest as inefficient token usage in multi-agent conversations, where agents like group chats and speaker selection loops generate excessive off-topic dialogue or redundant consensus cycles. Common failure modes such as spiraling conversations and biased speaker selection amplify costs by triggering unnecessary LLM calls. Vex integration via message hooks provides real-time monitoring of these cost leaks specific to AG2's enhanced group chat patterns.

howItManifests.scenario: Agent conversation spiraling off-topic in sequential chat
howItManifests.symptom: Agents exceed 50+ message turns discussing irrelevant tangents
howItManifests.impact: Token costs increase 10x due to continuous LLM inference cycles

howItManifests.scenario: Group chat consensus loops on incorrect facts
howItManifests.symptom: All agents repeatedly vote on same wrong answer (>20 rounds)
howItManifests.impact: $5+ per conversation from redundant termination checks

howItManifests.scenario: Speaker selection bias in multi-agent groups
howItManifests.symptom: Single agent dominates 80%+ of speaking turns
howItManifests.impact: Uneven token distribution wastes budget on repetitive speakers

howItManifests.scenario: Code execution agents with verbose debugging
howItManifests.symptom: Agents generate 2k+ token code explanations per iteration
howItManifests.impact: Development tasks cost $2-10 per simple function creation

detection.name: Message Turn Counter
detection.description: Tracks conversation length and flags cost explosions from long-running chats

detection.name: Speaker Dominance Monitor
detection.description: Detects when single agents monopolize conversation bandwidth

detection.name: Token Velocity Tracker
detection.description: Measures tokens per minute to catch accelerating cost burn
detection.code:
import time
from ag2 import autogen

def token_velocity_detector(messages, agent):
    if len(messages) < 10:
        return None
    
    recent_tokens = sum(len(str(m.content)) for m in messages[-10:])
    time_span = (messages[-1].timestamp - messages[-10].timestamp).total_seconds()
    velocity = recent_tokens / max(time_span, 60)
    
    if velocity > 5000:  # 5k tokens/minute threshold
        return {
            'alert': 'TOKEN_VELOCITY_EXCESS',
            'velocity_tpm': velocity,
            'projected_hourly_cost': velocity * 60 * 0.0001  # $0.10/1M tokens
        }
    return None

fixes.name: Max Turns Termination
fixes.description: Force-end conversations after cost threshold exceeded

fixes.name: Speaker Rotation Enforcement
fixes.description: Mandate turn-taking to prevent dominance bias
fixes.code:
from collections import deque
from ag2 import GroupChatManager

class FairSpeakerSelector:
    def __init__(self, agents):
        self.speakers = deque(agents)
        self.recent_speakers = set()
    
    def select_speaker(self):
        # Skip if spoke recently
        while self.speakers[0] in self.recent_speakers:
            self.speakers.rotate(-1)
        speaker = self.speakers[0]
        self.recent_speakers.add(speaker)
        if len(self.recent_speakers) > 3:
            self.recent_speakers.clear()
        return speaker

# Usage
selector = FairSpeakerSelector(group_chat.agents)
group_chat.select_speaker = selector.select_speaker

fixes.name: Token Budget Guardrails
fixes.code:
class TokenBudgetAgent:
    def __init__(self, budget=10000):
        self.budget = budget
        self.used = 0
    
    async def before_message(self, message):
        projected = len(str(message.content))
        if self.used + projected > self.budget:
            message.content = message.content[:500] + "\n[TRUNCATED: Budget limit reached]"
        self.used += len(str(message.content))
        return message

# Register with AG2
budget_guard = TokenBudgetAgent()
group_chat.message_hooks.append(budget_guard.before_message)

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Cost Optimization Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates directly as AG2 message hooks to monitor token consumption patterns across all conversation types. It automatically detects spiraling dialogues, speaker imbalances, and velocity spikes using the exact detection methods above, providing real-time cost projections and automated remediation.

The Vex SDK (`pip install vex-sdk`) registers as a zero-overhead observer on AG2's message bus, capturing metadata like speaker turns, token counts, and conversation state without interrupting agent flow. When cost anomalies trigger (e.g., >5k TPM or 70% speaker dominance), Vex can auto-apply fixes like turn limits or budget truncation.

AG2-specific patterns like group chat consensus loops and code agent verbosity are pre-configured in Vex monitors, reducing false positives by 90% compared to generic observability.
vexAutomation.code:
from vex_sdk import VexMonitor
from ag2 import GroupChat

# Install: pip install ag2 vex-sdk

vex = VexMonitor(
    project_id="your-vex-project",
    detectors=[
        "cost_turn_counter",
        "speaker_dominance",
        "token_velocity"
    ],
    auto_fixes=[
        "max_turns_termination",
        "speaker_rotation",
        "token_budget"
    ]
)

# Zero-config AG2 integration
group_chat = GroupChat(agents=[...])
group_chat.register_hook(vex.message_observer)

# Vex auto-generates cost dashboard and alerts
# Monitors catch 95% of cost leaks before $1 burn
print(f"Vex monitoring active. Budget protection: ${vex.daily_budget}")

group_chat.initiate_chat()
